skill.name: py-observability
skill.description: Observability patterns for Python backends. Use when adding logging, metrics, tracing, or debugging production issues.
skill.summary: Add structured logging, metrics, tracing, health checks, and redaction patterns to Python backend services.
skill.author: CJHarmath

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer flagged Markdown code fences as shell execution and sensitive key names as environment access; both are false positives. No prompt injection, executable scripts, network exfiltration, or malicious intent was found. Low risk remains because examples mention logging SQL parameters and sending user email to Sentry.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Finding: Markdown Code Fences Misread as External Commands
low_findings.description: The reported external command locations are Python Markdown code fences and example snippets. They do not instruct the model to execute shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines are Markdown code block delimiters or boundaries around Python examples. No shell command syntax or execution instruction appears at those locations.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Finding: Sensitive Key Names Used for Redaction
low_findings.description: The reported secret-key locations define names that should be scrubbed before logging. They do not read environment variables or expose credentials.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Both lines list sensitive key names inside redaction logic. The surrounding examples replace matching values with a redacted marker.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Finding: Weak Cryptography Pattern Not Present
low_findings.description: No evidence of a weak cryptographic algorithm was found at the reported lines. The lines contain metadata or logging context comments.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited text does not call MD5, SHA1, DES, RC4, or any other cryptographic API. This appears to be a substring match.

low_findings.title: Dismissed Static Finding: System Reconnaissance Pattern Not Present
low_findings.description: The reported locations contain UUID generation, comments, and checklist items for tracing application requests. They do not perform host reconnaissance.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The context is request correlation and observability checklists. No commands such as uname, whoami, ipconfig, or network probing are present.

low_findings.title: Privacy Caution in Telemetry Examples
low_findings.description: The examples show SQL parameters in debug logs and user email in Sentry context. Implementers should remove or redact sensitive values before production use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The examples are instructional, not executable skill code, but they could lead users to send sensitive data to logs or Sentry.

content.value_statement: Python services are difficult to operate when logs, metrics, and traces are inconsistent. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code practical patterns for production observability.
actual_capabilities: Adds structured logging patterns with request and business context.
Defines Prometheus counters, histograms, gauges, and metrics endpoints.
Shows Sentry setup with integrations, sampling, and redaction hooks.
Adds request IDs and flow IDs for tracing multi-step operations.
Provides health check and readiness check examples for FastAPI services.
Includes sensitive data redaction patterns for log payloads.
limitations: Examples require adaptation to each service framework and data model.
The skill does not install packages or configure infrastructure by itself.
Metric names, buckets, and labels should be reviewed for each workload.
Teams must validate privacy requirements before sending data to external tools.

use_cases.title: Instrument a New API
use_cases.description: Add request logging, metrics, health checks, and redaction before a Python API reaches production.

use_cases.title: Improve Incident Debugging
use_cases.description: Add request IDs, flow IDs, and contextual errors so failures can be traced across logs and Sentry.

use_cases.title: Standardize Observability Reviews
use_cases.description: Use the checklist to review whether a feature emits useful logs, metrics, and alerts.

prompt_templates.title: Add Basic Logging
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to add structured logging to my Python endpoint. Include request ID, user ID, operation name, and error context.

prompt_templates.title: Add Metrics
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to add Prometheus metrics for request count, latency, failures, and key business events in this FastAPI service.

prompt_templates.title: Trace a Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to add flow tracing to this multi-step Python workflow. Include start, step, success, and failure logs.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Observability
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced review of an existing service
prompt_templates.prompt: Use this skill to audit the observability in this Python backend. Identify missing logs, unsafe telemetry, missing metrics, and weak health checks.

output_examples.input: Add production observability to a FastAPI endpoint.
output: A structured logging plan with request context and safe error fields.
Prometheus metrics for request volume, latency, and failures.
A health check and readiness check matched to service dependencies.

output_examples.input: Review why an assessment submission flow is hard to debug.
output: A list of missing flow checkpoints and silent returns.
Suggested flow IDs and event names for each operation step.
Redaction guidance for user, request, and error data.

output_examples.input: Prepare Sentry error tracking for a Python service.
output: Recommended Sentry integration settings and sampling notes.
A privacy review checklist for user context and request payloads.
Guidance for adding tags and context without exposing secrets.

best_practices: Redact sensitive fields before logging request bodies, parameters, or user context.
Use stable metric labels to avoid high-cardinality Prometheus data.
Add logs and metrics at operation boundaries, not only inside exception handlers.
anti_patterns: Do not log raw tokens, passwords, authorization headers, or full request payloads.
Do not create metrics with user IDs, emails, or unbounded path values as labels.
Do not rely on health endpoints that ignore database, cache, or queue dependencies.

faq.question: What frameworks does this skill target?
faq.answer: The examples focus on Python backends, especially FastAPI services using structlog, Prometheus, SQLAlchemy, and Sentry.

faq.question: Does the skill run commands or install packages?
faq.answer: No. It provides implementation patterns and example code for an AI assistant to adapt.

faq.question: Can it help with existing production incidents?
faq.answer: Yes. It can guide log correlation, flow tracing, metrics review, and Sentry context improvements.

faq.question: Does it prevent sensitive data leaks automatically?
faq.answer: No. It includes redaction patterns, but teams must review telemetry against their data policies.

faq.question: Can I use it with Claude Code or Codex?
faq.answer: Yes. The skill is compatible with Claude, Claude Code, and Codex workflows.

faq.question: What should I customize first?
faq.answer: Start with event names, metric labels, sampling rates, and redaction rules for your service.
